props.text: MiroShark's public gallery now has a credibility surface. Any operator who publishes a simulation can mark its real-world outcome — "Called it" / "Partial" / "Called wrong", plus an optional source URL and a one-sentence summary — straight from the Embed dialog. The annotation immediately surfaces as a 📍 Verified pill on the gallery card and on a brand-new /verified page that lists only the runs that called real events.

Yesterday Bankr Terminal v2 cited MiroShark's Aave vulnerability simulation in a 15M-view thread — the strongest external signal in the project's history, and the moment that frames MiroShark as an early-warning system rather than a simulation demo. That citation lived on X and nowhere in the product. With /verified, every researcher who calls an event early gets a permanent home for that proof — and visitors evaluating the project land on a page full of real predictions instead of having to take screenshots on faith. For the 1K-stars-by-Apr-30 sprint, /verified is the single most compelling link to drop into a thread about pre-incident simulations.

backend/app/api/simulation.py: New POST/GET /api/simulation/<id>/outcome endpoints (publish-gated on POST, read-only GET) writing <sim_dir>/outcome.json. _read_outcome_file() helper validates the correct/incorrect/partial vocabulary, truncates oversized summaries with an ellipsis, and strips any non-http URL — so a corrupt artifact can never land a javascript: value on the gallery. _build_gallery_card_payload() now surfaces an `outcome` field on every card. /api/simulation/public gains a `?verified=1` filter that paginates the verified set and reflects `verified_only` back in the response.

frontend/src/views/ExploreView.vue: New "Verified only" filter chip in the stats row (toggles between /explore and /verified URLs so the view is shareable). 📍/⚠/◑ outcome pills on each card, clickable when an outcome URL was provided. Coloured left-edge accent on cards with a verified outcome. Dynamic page title/copy/empty-state when in verified-only mode + a `verifiedCount` stat chip.

frontend/src/components/EmbedDialog.vue: New "Mark outcome" panel — 3-way radio group (Called it / Partial / Called wrong), URL input, 280-char textarea with live counter, save/update button, success/error inline message, "View on /verified ↗" once saved. Inputs disabled until the simulation is public.
frontend/src/router/index.js: New /verified route mapped to ExploreView with `verifiedOnly: true`.
backend/openapi.yaml: New path + SimulationOutcome schema + `outcome` field on GalleryCard + `verified` query param + `verified_only` response field. Drift-detection test continues to pass.

backend/tests/test_unit_outcome.py: 8 offline unit tests covering valid record parsing, unknown-label rejection, corrupt-JSON swallowing, missing-file behaviour, oversized-summary truncation, non-http URL stripping, and gallery-card integration with and without an outcome.
docs/FEATURES.md + README.md: New Verified Predictions section + feature row.

The outcome.json artifact is intentionally a sibling of the existing resolution.json — /resolve is binary YES/NO and tied to Polymarket consensus accuracy_score, /outcome is open-ended text + a label + an optional URL aimed at the gallery. A simulation can have both. The /verified URL renders the same Vue component as /explore but boots the filter on via a route prop, and the filter chip on /explore replaces the URL between the two paths (router.replace) so the page is shareable and bookmarkable in either state. The pill component switches between <a> and <span> at runtime depending on whether an outcome URL was provided — clickable when there's a destination, plain pill otherwise.

Ideas #2 (Animated GIF Export) and #3 (Share as Thread Formatter) from yesterday's repo-actions both build naturally on this — every verified sim becomes a stronger candidate for both formats. A "Most accurate operator" leaderboard (#2 from Apr 24's batch) is now also unlocked: the ledger has the data it needs.
